channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Daniel Noboa is reelected Ecuador's president by voters weary of crime
item.description: QUITO, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuadorian voters weary of crime reelected President Daniel Noboa, a conservative young millionaire with a divisive no-holds-barred crimefighting record, by a wide margin Sunday, but his opponent vowed to seek a recount over what she described as “grotesque” electoral fraud.
item.dc:creator: GONZALO SOLANO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Retrial opens for Sarah Palin's libel suit against The New York Times
item.description: A retrial is set to begin for Sarah Palin's lawsuit accusing The New York Times of libeling her in an editorial eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Boxing legend George Foreman to be honored at Houston memorial service
item.description: Family, friends and the public are preparing to remember boxing legend, pastor and entrepreneur George Foreman during a memorial service in his hometown of Houston.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FAA says company whose sightseeing chopper crashed, killing 6, is ceasing operations immediately
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Federal Aviation Administration said Sunday that the helicopter tour company whose sightseeing chopper broke apart in flight and crashed in New York, killing the pilot and a family of five visitors from Spain, is shutting down operations immediately.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: State Department wants staff to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias during Biden's term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Christian world commemorates Holy Week leading up to Easter Sunday, the State Department has issued an appeal for its employees to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias, including formal or informal actions due to opposition to vaccines or personal pronoun choice,
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The dead in upstate New York plane crash included 2022 NCAA woman of the year and family members
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 cases of rare brain disease reported in Oregon
item.description: Three cases of a rare brain disease have been reported by public health officials in Hood River County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Flanigan, KTLA 5

item.title: MISSING: 12-year-old girl considered endangered after running away from home
item.description: A Utah Endangered Missing Advisory has been activated after a 12-year-old girl reportedly ran away from her South Salt Lake home on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: 6 injured after explosion, house collapse in Austin
item.description: One house was "leveled" while another "suffered severe collapse damage" following an explosion in Austin on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, KRON4

item.title: A beloved pet tortoise is reunited with its family weeks after disappearing in a Mississippi tornado
item.description: KOKOMO, Miss. (AP) — Myrtle, a cherished pet tortoise, has been reunited with its family in Mississippi weeks after disappearing during a deadly tornado outbreak in March. “He’s been through a lot,” said Myrtle's grateful owner, Tiffany Emanuel.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nicky Katt of 'Dazed and Confused,' 'Boston Public' dies: reports
item.description: Nicky Katt, who was known for his roles in "Dazed and Confused" and "Boiler Room," has died, according to multiple reports. He was 54 years old.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect arrested in arson fire that forced Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro, family to flee residence
item.description: Police say a man has been arrested after a suspected arson fire at the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion while Democratic Gov. Josh Shapiro and his family slept inside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: In a report released Sunday, the doctor said in a summary that Trump is “fully fit to execute the duties of Commander-in-Chief and Head of State.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fire at Pennsylvania Governor's Residence was 'act of arson': State Police
item.description: Police said Governor Josh Shapiro and his family were able to safely evacuate after a fire — believed to be an "act of arson" — broke out at the Harrisburg mansion.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KRON4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on how flat funding set stage for Texas measles outbreak, might fuel more
item.description: The measles outbreak in West Texas didn’t happen just by chance. The easily preventable disease, declared eliminated in the U.S. in 2000, ripped through Texas communities in part because health departments were starved of the funding needed to run vaccine programs, officials say.
item.dc:creator: LAURA UNGAR, MICHELLE R. SMITH and DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Understanding mutual funds: A beginner's guide to investing
item.description: Despite recent stock market slumps and fears in the investment world, it's never a bad time to learn about strategies to boost your investing experience.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Viral CIA file about aliens attacking soldiers takes off with UFO intrigue
item.description: A Soviet-era document published on the CIA's website describes a retaliatory attack by aliens after soldiers shot down a UFO nearly 30 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What we know about the New York City helicopter crash that killed 6 people
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River near the New Jersey shoreline, killing the pilot and a Spanish family of five who were on board.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US still won't say whether it will return mistakenly deported man, despite Supreme Court decision
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its decision not to tell a federal court whether it has any plans to repatriate a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month and remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DHS ends protections from deportation for Afghanistan, Cameroon
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has stripped protections from deportation for Afghans and Cameroonians in the U.S., calling into question the ability of some Afghan evacuees to remain in America.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Prince Harry makes visit to Ukraine, meets with wounded veterans
item.description: The Duke of Sussex visited the Superhumans Center in Lviv on Thursday, a spokesperson confirmed to ITK.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Augusta man attends 62 Masters in a row
item.description: For many people, they’ll wait their whole life to go to The Masters. For others, they’ve been going their whole life.
item.dc:creator: Karlton Clay, Fox 5

item.title: Minecraft 'Chicken Jockey' trend hits Utah theaters, movie-goer kicked out after bringing live chicken
item.description: Videos online show crowds watching 'A Minecraft Movie' in theaters cheering wildly as part of a viral trend — but, after a recent event with streamers, air horns, and a live chicken in a Utah theater, some are saying the trend has gone too far.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Gas explosion causes $3 million in damages at Spanish Fork dental office
item.description: A gas explosion caused an estimated $3 million in damages at a Spanish Fork dental office Friday morning, according to Spanish Fork Fire & EMS.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Schools lined up for help getting cleaner school buses. Then came the EPA freeze
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Looking to cut pollution from its fleet of diesel-burning school buses, an Oklahoma school district last year doubled its electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: Feds say agents went to LA schools to do welfare check on migrant children
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Homeland Security officers visited two Los Angeles public elementary schools this week to do a welfare check on migrant children, not for immigration enforcement, the department said Friday. The officers were denied access by both principals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida universities to deputize campus police for immigration enforcement
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The police departments of at least three public universities in Florida are seeking agreements with the federal government to carry out immigration enforcement on campus.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tufts student from Turkey details arrest, crowded detention conditions in new court filing
item.description: A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey is demanding her release after she was detained by immigration officials near her Massachusetts home, detailing how she was scared when the men grabbed her phone and feared she would be killed.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon’s network includes millions of third-party sellers who are either in China or whose products originate from there.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: Six people were killed Thursday in the latest string of aviation disasters after a sightseeing helicopter broke apart and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9
